components.name: sodium nitrate (NaNO3)
components.evidence: BG-11 medium is composed of (in 1 L): 1.5 g NaNO3

components.name: boric acid (H3BO3)
components.evidence: 1 mL BG-11 trace metals solution. The BG-11 trace metals solution (in 1 L) consists of: 2.86 g H3BO3 [diluted 1000x]

components.name: manganese(II) chloride tetrahydrate (MnCl2·4H2O)
components.evidence: 1.81 g MnCl2·4H2O [in trace metals solution, 1 mL/L]

components.name: zinc sulfate heptahydrate (ZnSO4·7H2O)
components.evidence: 0.22 g ZnSO4·7H2O [in trace metals solution, 1 mL/L]

components.name: sodium molybdate dihydrate (Na2MoO4·2H2O)
components.evidence: 0.39 g Na2MoO4·2H2O [in trace metals solution, 1 mL/L]

components.name: copper(II) sulfate pentahydrate (CuSO4·5H2O)
components.evidence: 0.079 g CuSO4·5H2O [in trace metals solution, 1 mL/L]

components.name: cobalt(II) nitrate hexahydrate (Co(NO3)2·6H2O)
components.evidence: 49.4 mg Co(NO3)2·6H2O [in trace metals solution, 1 mL/L]

components.name: sodium chloride (NaCl)
components.evidence: supplemented with 1 g L−1 HEPES (pH 8.9) and 100 mM NaCl at 35 °C

media.base_medium: BG-11 (Sigma–Aldrich) cyanobacterial medium, supplemented with HEPES and NaCl

sources_chased.how: read main_text Methods (Strains and culturing conditions) — paper spells out the complete recipe including the trace metals solution, so no external reference chase was required

media.notes: Complete standard BG-11 recipe is given verbatim in the paper's Methods, including the full trace-metals solution (B, Mn, Zn, Mo, Cu, Co) added at 1 mL/L, so trace-element concentrations are the stock values divided by 1000. Boron and the carbonate/EDTA/citrate chelator system were absent from current_components and are now added. Ferric ammonium citrate has undefined stoichiometry (concentration_mM null); it supplies both the Fe3+ and the ammonium seen in current_components. Medium is aerobic photoautotrophic (illuminated 24 h, shaking 150 rpm); target had listed oxygen as facultative reflecting the downstream phototroph/heterotroph coculture use.
